changes.value:
## What
Connect the `app.search` command (already a placeholder in `app-shell.tsx`) to open the command palette in search mode. Add keybindings for `/` (vim) and `Mod+F` (CUA/emacs).

**Files:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx`, `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts`

**Approach:**
- In `app-shell.tsx`: add `paletteMode` state ("command" | "search"), update `app.search` execute to set mode to "search" and open palette, update `app.command`/`app.palette` to set mode to "command"
- Pass `mode` prop to `CommandPalette`
- In `keybindings.ts`: add `"/": "app.search"` to vim bindings, `"Mod+F": "app.search"` to cua and emacs bindings

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pressing `/` in vim mode opens palette in search mode
- [ ] Pressing `Cmd+F` in CUA/emacs mode opens palette in search mode
- [ ] Pressing `:` in vim still opens command mode
- [ ] Pressing `Cmd+Shift+P` still opens command mode
- [ ] Escape closes the palette regardless of mode

## Tests
- [ ] Existing keybinding tests still pass
- [ ] Manual: verify all keybinding combos open correct mode

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,20 +1,29 @@
 ## What
-Connect the `app.search` command (already a placeholder in `app-shell.tsx`) to open the command palette in search mode. Add keybindings for `/` (vim) and `Mod+F` (CUA/emacs).
+Make `app.search` a proper command in the command handling system — registered in YAML command definitions (like `app.command` and `app.palette`), with keybindings defined in the YAML, and dispatched through the scope chain. The frontend `execute` handler opens the palette in search mode.
+
+Currently `app.search` is a placeholder in the `globalCommands` array in `app-shell.tsx` with an empty `execute`. It needs to:
+1. Be defined in the builtin command YAML (`ui.yaml` or `app.yaml`) so it flows through the registry
+2. Have keybindings defined in YAML (`/` for vim, `Cmd+F` for CUA/emacs)
+3. Have a frontend `execute` handler in `app-shell.tsx` that sets palette mode to "search" and opens it

-**Files:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx`, `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts`
+**Files:**
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/*.yaml` — add `app.search` command definition with keys
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add `paletteMode` state, update `app.search` execute handler, pass mode to CommandPalette
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` — add `/` (vim) and `Mod+F` (CUA/emacs) to BINDING_TABLES for `app.search`

 **Approach:**
-- In `app-shell.tsx`: add `paletteMode` state ("command" | "search"), update `app.search` execute to set mode to "search" and open palette, update `app.command`/`app.palette` to set mode to "command"
-- Pass `mode` prop to `CommandPalette`
-- In `keybindings.ts`: add `"/": "app.search"` to vim bindings, `"Mod+F": "app.search"` to cua and emacs bindings
+- Follow the same pattern as `app.command` / `app.palette` — defined in YAML, keybindings in both YAML and the binding tables, frontend execute handler opens the palette
+- `app.search` is a UI command (has frontend execute), not a Rust-side command
+- NavBar search button dispatches `app.search` through `useExecuteCommand()` from the scope chain — same path as keyboard

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Pressing `/` in vim mode opens palette in search mode
-- [ ] Pressing `Cmd+F` in CUA/emacs mode opens palette in search mode
-- [ ] Pressing `:` in vim still opens command mode
-- [ ] Pressing `Cmd+Shift+P` still opens command mode
-- [ ] Escape closes the palette regardless of mode
+- [ ] `app.search` appears in the command registry (listable, inspectable)
+- [ ] Pressing `/` in vim mode dispatches `app.search` through scope chain
+- [ ] Pressing `Cmd+F` in CUA/emacs dispatches `app.search` through scope chain
+- [ ] Command palette opens in search mode when `app.search` fires
+- [ ] `app.search` shows up in the command palette itself (searchable by name)
+- [ ] NavBar search button uses `useExecuteCommand("app.search")` — same dispatch path

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Existing keybinding tests still pass
-- [ ] Manual: verify all keybinding combos open correct mode
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: verify all keybinding combos and NavBar button open search mode via command dispatch
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,29 +1,30 @@
 ## What
-Make `app.search` a proper command in the command handling system — registered in YAML command definitions (like `app.command` and `app.palette`), with keybindings defined in the YAML, and dispatched through the scope chain. The frontend `execute` handler opens the palette in search mode.
+Make `app.search` a proper command in the command handling system. Dispatching `app.search` (no args) means "launch the search UI" — opens the command palette in search mode.

-Currently `app.search` is a placeholder in the `globalCommands` array in `app-shell.tsx` with an empty `execute`. It needs to:
-1. Be defined in the builtin command YAML (`ui.yaml` or `app.yaml`) so it flows through the registry
-2. Have keybindings defined in YAML (`/` for vim, `Cmd+F` for CUA/emacs)
+Currently `app.search` is a placeholder in `globalCommands` in `app-shell.tsx` with an empty `execute`. It needs to:
+1. Be defined in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml` (alongside `ui.palette.open`)
+2. Have keybindings: `/` for vim, `Mod+F` for CUA/emacs
 3. Have a frontend `execute` handler in `app-shell.tsx` that sets palette mode to "search" and opens it

 **Files:**
-- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/*.yaml` — add `app.search` command definition with keys
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add `paletteMode` state, update `app.search` execute handler, pass mode to CommandPalette
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` — add `/` (vim) and `Mod+F` (CUA/emacs) to BINDING_TABLES for `app.search`
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml` — add `app.search` command definition with keys
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add `paletteMode` state ("command" | "search"), update `app.search` execute handler, pass mode to CommandPalette
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` — add `"/": "app.search"` to vim, `"Mod+F": "app.search"` to CUA/emacs binding tables

 **Approach:**
-- Follow the same pattern as `app.command` / `app.palette` — defined in YAML, keybindings in both YAML and the binding tables, frontend execute handler opens the palette
-- `app.search` is a UI command (has frontend execute), not a Rust-side command
-- NavBar search button dispatches `app.search` through `useExecuteCommand()` from the scope chain — same path as keyboard
+- Follow same pattern as `ui.palette.open` — defined in YAML, frontend execute handler
+- `app.search` with no args = launch search UI (open palette in search mode)
+- NavBar search button dispatches `app.search` through `useExecuteCommand()` — same path as keyboard
+- Closing the palette resets mode back to "command"

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `app.search` appears in the command registry (listable, inspectable)
-- [ ] Pressing `/` in vim mode dispatches `app.search` through scope chain
-- [ ] Pressing `Cmd+F` in CUA/emacs dispatches `app.search` through scope chain
-- [ ] Command palette opens in search mode when `app.search` fires
-- [ ] `app.search` shows up in the command palette itself (searchable by name)
-- [ ] NavBar search button uses `useExecuteCommand("app.search")` — same dispatch path
+- [ ] `app.search` defined in ui.yaml with keys for all modes
+- [ ] Dispatching `app.search` (no args) opens palette in search mode
+- [ ] `/` (vim) and `Cmd+F` (CUA/emacs) trigger `app.search` through scope chain
+- [ ] `app.search` shows up in command palette (searchable by name "Search")
+- [ ] NavBar search button uses `useExecuteCommand("app.search")`

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Existing keybinding tests still pass
-- [ ] Manual: verify all keybinding combos and NavBar button open search mode via command dispatch
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Existing keybinding tests pass
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` (YAML parses)
+- [ ] Manual: all keybinding combos and NavBar button open search mode
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,30 +1,30 @@
 ## What
-Make `app.search` a proper command in the command handling system. Dispatching `app.search` (no args) means "launch the search UI" — opens the command palette in search mode.
+Make the existing `app.search` placeholder in `app-shell.tsx` functional. This is a **frontend-only** command (same pattern as `app.command`, `app.palette`, `app.dismiss`) — it stays in the `globalCommands` array with an `execute` handler. It does NOT go in `ui.yaml` because `app.*` commands are frontend-scoped, not Rust-dispatched.
+
+**Architecture note:** The codebase has two command namespaces:
+- `app.*` — frontend-only, defined in `app-shell.tsx` globalCommands with local `execute` handlers
+- `ui.*` — Rust-side, defined in `ui.yaml` with `Command` trait impls

-Currently `app.search` is a placeholder in `globalCommands` in `app-shell.tsx` with an empty `execute`. It needs to:
-1. Be defined in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml` (alongside `ui.palette.open`)
-2. Have keybindings: `/` for vim, `Mod+F` for CUA/emacs
-3. Have a frontend `execute` handler in `app-shell.tsx` that sets palette mode to "search" and opens it
+`app.search` is purely a UI action (open palette in search mode), so it follows the `app.*` pattern.

 **Files:**
-- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml` — add `app.search` command definition with keys
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add `paletteMode` state ("command" | "search"), update `app.search` execute handler, pass mode to CommandPalette
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add `paletteMode` state ("command" | "search"), fill in `app.search` execute handler, pass mode to CommandPalette
 - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` — add `"/": "app.search"` to vim, `"Mod+F": "app.search"` to CUA/emacs binding tables

 **Approach:**
-- Follow same pattern as `ui.palette.open` — defined in YAML, frontend execute handler
-- `app.search` with no args = launch search UI (open palette in search mode)
-- NavBar search button dispatches `app.search` through `useExecuteCommand()` — same path as keyboard
-- Closing the palette resets mode back to "command"
+- Update existing `app.search` placeholder's `execute` to: `setPaletteOpen(true); setPaletteMode("search"); setMode("command");`
+- Update `app.command` / `app.palette` execute to also: `setPaletteMode("command")`
+- Pass `paletteMode` to `CommandPalette` as `mode` prop
+- Closing palette resets mode to "command"
+- NavBar search button dispatches `app.search` through `useExecuteCommand()` — same dispatch path as keyboard

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `app.search` defined in ui.yaml with keys for all modes
-- [ ] Dispatching `app.search` (no args) opens palette in search mode
+- [ ] `app.search` has a working execute handler in globalCommands
 - [ ] `/` (vim) and `Cmd+F` (CUA/emacs) trigger `app.search` through scope chain
-- [ ] `app.search` shows up in command palette (searchable by name "Search")
+- [ ] `:` (vim) and `Cmd+Shift+P` still open command mode
+- [ ] `app.search` shows in command palette (searchable by name "Search")
 - [ ] NavBar search button uses `useExecuteCommand("app.search")`

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Existing keybinding tests pass
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` (YAML parses)
-- [ ] Manual: all keybinding combos and NavBar button open search mode
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: all keybinding combos open correct mode
\ No newline at end of file

It does NOT go in `ui.yaml` because `app.*` commands are frontend-scoped, not Rust-dispatched.
+Make `app.search` a proper command in the command handling system. Dispatching `app.search` (no args) means "launch the search UI" — opens the command palette in search mode.

-**Architecture note:** The codebase has two command namespaces:

 **Files:**

+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — add `paletteMode` state ("command" | "search"), update `app.search` execute handler, pass mode to CommandPalette

+- NavBar search button dispatches `app.search` through `useExecuteCommand()` — same path as keyboard

 ## Acceptance Criteria

+- [ ] `app.search` defined in ui.yaml with keys for all modes

 - [ ] `/` (vim) and `Cmd+F` (CUA/emacs) trigger `app.search` through scope chain
-- [ ] `:` (vim) and `Cmd+Shift+P` still open command mode
-- [ ] `app.search` shows in command palette (searchable by name "Search")
+- [ ] `app.search` shows up in command palette (searchable by name "Search")
 - [ ] NavBar search button uses `useExecuteCommand("app.search")`

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Existing keybinding tests pass
-- [ ] Manual: all keybinding combos open correct mode
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` (YAML parses)
+- [ ] Manual: all keybinding combos and NavBar button open search mode
\ No newline at end of file
